input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` MSBuild property, which is set to `true` automatically by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- MTP properties — only for test projects -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (which every MSTest project already references) sets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Production/library projects never get that property, so the conditional group is skipped for them.

**Key points:**
- No naming conventions or manual flags needed.
- If a project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, it's automatically opted in.
- `EnableMSTestRunner` enables the MTP runner; `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` enables `dotnet test` compatibility with MTP.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally apply MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that certain properties (like `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and MTP runner properties) only apply to test projects, not production/library projects.

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conventions. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` or uses a naming convention. But the most robust and commonly recommended approach for MTP migration is to condition on `IsTestProject`.
